violations.text: Cooked ground beef was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit in 1-door reach-in #2. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food item was voluntarily disposed of during the time of inspection. Food item was observed on bottom of refrigerator, discussed with PIC to add shelf clips and adjust height of shelf so that air flow can circulate under food items. Food item was voluntarily disposed of during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on 2-door reach-in unit #2. Refrigerator door gaskets must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the air circulating fans have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on both reach-in units. Refrigerator door gaskets must be maintained in good repair Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair on both reach-in units. Refrigerator door gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2024

The doors to the outside, located in the kitchen and cafeteria, are not tight-fitting. Space observed at the bottom of the doors. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2024
Refrigerator door gasket of the stainless 1-door reach-in observed in poor repair. Gasket is to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2024
Faucet of the handsink observed to be leaking. Plumbing must be maintained in good repair. Faucet is to be repaired/replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment shall register a ServSafe certified employee with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as the Certified Food Safety Manager (RI license). New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023

The door to the outside, located in the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2023

The door to the outside, located in the front and back, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Per interview of staff, an order has been placed for a new back door. *This violation has not exceed the original correction date of 07/31/23. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2023

Grilled chicken and chicken nuggets observed held at 117-120 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. -Water in the steam table observed very low. Since staff had finished serving lunch, the foods were voluntarily disposed of during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023

Beans observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit and stored on top of melt ice packs on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. -Per interview of staff, beans were held less than 2 hours. -Staff moved the beans to the refrigerator during this inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. -Per interview of staff, an order has been placed for a new door. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the fans have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. The non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent buildup of residue. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2023
